# Amazon River

The Amazon River in South America is the largest river in the world by discharge,
carrying more water than the next several largest rivers combined. It is roughly
6,400 kilometers long, making it among the longest rivers on Earth. The Amazon
originates in the Andes of Peru and empties into the Atlantic Ocean in Brazil.
Its drainage basin, the Amazon basin, contains the world's largest rainforest.
The river has no bridges crossing its main stem.
